_source: atomic-copy — the site's user-facing UI chrome as addressable copy atoms (the verbal counterpart to design tokens; see docs/atomic-copy.md). Every static, human-readable string in build.mjs's templates that is NOT sourced from the content contracts (data/profile.json identity/résumé copy, data/presentation.json render-context slugs, data/site.json corpus) lives here, keyed by a stable dotted id and referenced via copy(id) in build.mjs. An unknown id throws and fails the build (like posts.mjs {{token}} transclusion); scripts/copy-gate.mjs scans the rendered homepage, résumé, /provenance, /blog (index + posts) and the <head> meta (titles/descriptions) for any visible word not traceable to an atom. A few atoms carry inline emphasis markup or a literal HTML entity (e.g. prov.chain.lede's <strong>/<em>, blog.nav.rss's  , prov.step.contracts' ·) — that is the EXACT rendered chrome string, kept verbatim for byte-identical output. Validated against contract/copy.schema.json.
colophon.more: Hermetic Nix build · keyless-signed in-toto/SLSA provenance (Sigstore · Rekor · GHCR) —
colophon.title: Colophon
colophon.lede: The upstream tools that produce and validate this site — each a real build input or gate, not a credit list. See /provenance for the full signed chain.
blog.lede: On capability security for agentic systems — the thesis, graded against the running code.
prov.title: Provenance
prov.lede: This site is built deterministically from versioned sources and validated at every boundary — the same discipline the work itself argues for. Here's what produced and checked this artifact.
prov.chain.lede: The build reads as an in-toto / SLSA-style provenance: declared materials, a checked build process, and a signed subject. Each link is verified; the last is the artifact itself.
prov.seal.title: Subject — signed
conf.title: Conformance
conf.lede: A live, computed conformance report — not a badge. The build folds its own evidence (the gates that ran, the artifacts it signs) through lone's web-build conformance model. The strong claim is withheld unless every required criterion is met; anything not yet assessed says so, plainly.
head.resume.label: Résumé
head.provenance.desc: How robertdelanghe.dev is built and validated — contracts, gates, and claim-to-evidence.
head.conformance.desc: A computed, honest conformance report for robertdelanghe.dev — lone's web-build model over this build's evidence; unsupplied criteria are reported as not-assessed, never overclaimed.
head.colophon.desc: The upstream tools that build and validate robertdelanghe.dev — each a real build input or gate.
head.blog.desc.tail: on capability security for agentic systems.
